Title: Ancient origin of fucosylated xyloglucan in charophycean green algae

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequences and transcriptomic data from Mesostigma viride, Chlorokybus atmophyticus, Chara braunii, Klebsormidium flaccidum, Spirogloea muscicola, Mesotaenium endlicherianum, and 1KP transcriptomes; GenBank sequences used for comparison
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CAZy GT2 cellulose synthase-like C family glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the β-(1,4)-glucan backbone of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer contributing to wall architecture and expansion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall, especially expanding/dividing cell wall domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred in late divergent CGA taxa producing XyG
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of CslC orthologs supports biosynthetic capacity for xyloglucan backbone formation in charophycean green algae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative phylogenetic analysis of GT2/Csl sequences; correlation with biochemical detection of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CGA sequences from Mesotaenium, Spirogloea, and Spirogyra clustered unambiguously in the CslC clade. 2. This supports that CGA synthesize a xyloglucan backbone using enzymes homologous to land-plant CslC proteins. 3. The finding underpins the ancient origin of xyloglucan in CGA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as orthologous machinery; no direct heterologous functional assay performed in this paper for CGA CslC proteins.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome/transcriptome mining and phylogenetic analysis of GT34 family sequences
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds α-(1,6)-linked xylose to the xyloglucan glucan backbone to initiate sidechain formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer contributing to wall architecture and expansion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred in Mesotaenium, Spirogyra, Cylindrocystis, and Spirogloea with identified GT34 homologs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of XXT-like GT34 orthologs supports the capacity of CGA to initiate xyloglucan sidechain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis of GT34 sequences with biochemical detection of xyloglucan sidechains
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GT34 orthologs were identified in Mesotaenium, Spirogyra, Cylindrocystis, and Spirogloea. 2. These enzymes likely catalyze the initiating xylosylation step of XyG sidechain synthesis. 3. Their presence supports a full XyG biosynthetic inventory in late divergent CGA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assignment is based on orthology; no direct enzymatic assay in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1KP transcriptomic data; cloned full-length cDNA sequence deposited in GenBank MW149251
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Sidechain extension by galactosylation/related GT47A decoration
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Galactosylated or otherwise GT47A-decorated xyloglucan sidechains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely transfers a sugar residue to O-2 of xylose in xyloglucan sidechains, analogous to XLT2/XST-related GT47A enzymes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer contributing to wall architecture and expansion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall of expanding/peripheral thallus cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cloned GT47A ortholog indicates genetic potential for GT47A-mediated xyloglucan sidechain elaboration in C. orbicularis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning, sequencing, phylogenetic placement relative to Arabidopsis XLT2 and tomato XST1/XST2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A full-length C. orbicularis GT47A gene, CoGT47A1, was cloned and deposited as MW149251. 2. It falls in a subclade subtending Arabidopsis XLT2 and tomato XST1/XST2. 3. This supports the presence of land-plant-like XyG sidechain biosynthetic machinery in CGA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The exact sugar donor and product specificity were not biochemically confirmed in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1KP transcriptomic data; cloned full-length cDNA sequence deposited in GenBank MW149252
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Sidechain extension by galactosylation/related GT47A decoration
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Galactosylated or otherwise GT47A-decorated xyloglucan sidechains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely transfers a sugar residue to O-2 of xylose in xyloglucan sidechains, analogous to XLT2/XST-related GT47A enzymes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer contributing to wall architecture and expansion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall of expanding/peripheral thallus cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cloned GT47A ortholog indicates genetic potential for GT47A-mediated xyloglucan sidechain elaboration in C. orbicularis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning, sequencing, phylogenetic placement relative to Arabidopsis XLT2 and tomato XST1/XST2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A second full-length C. orbicularis GT47A gene, CoGT47A2, was cloned and deposited as MW149252. 2. It clusters with GT47A enzymes implicated in XyG sidechain elaboration. 3. Together with CoGT47A1, it supports ancestral GT47A machinery in CGA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic activity remains inferred from phylogeny only.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome/transcriptome mining and phylogenetic analysis of GT37 sequences
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose onto galactosylated xyloglucan sidechains to form fucosylated XyG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer with specialized wall properties associated with expanding/dividing walls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall, especially expanding/dividing regions in Mesotaenium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GT37 orthologs in CGA support biosynthetic origin of fucosylated xyloglucan detected in Mesotaenium caldariorum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis of GT37 family plus biochemical validation of fucosylated xyloglucan by MALDI-ToF, fucosidase sensitivity, TLC co-migration with XXFG, and CCRC-M1/CCRC-M39 antibody labeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Several CGA GT37-type sequences were identified and cluster with putative XyG fucosyltransferases. 2. Mesotaenium caldariorum contains fucosylated XyG oligosaccharides such as XFG, XXFG, and XLFG. 3. These data indicate that XyG fucosylation predates land plants and was already present in charophycean green algae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The exact individual GT37 gene from Mesotaenium was not named or cloned in this paper; functional assignment relies on orthology plus product detection.
